messages.content:
Which of the following refers to any act of giving away, selling or distributing medicine or any dangerous drug with or without the use of
prescription?  base it on ph law
The act of giving away, selling or distributing medicine or any dangerous drug with or without the use of prescription is referred to as "drug trafficking" according to Philippine law.

According to the Philippine Pharmacy Law or Republic Act No. 10918, drug trafficking is defined as "the illegal manufacture, cultivation, sale, trading, administration, dispensation, delivery, distribution, transportation or importation of any dangerous drug and/or controlled precursor and essential chemical." This includes the act of giving away, selling, or distributing medicine or any dangerous drug without a valid prescription or proper authorization. Pharmacy professionals have a legal and ethical responsibility to ensure that drugs are dispensed and distributed only for legitimate, documented medical purposes and in accordance with the law.

Dispensing is the act of preparing and giving medications or drugs to a patient in a suitable form, accompanied by appropriate packaging, labeling, and patient education. In the context of pharmacy, dispensing involves interpreting prescriptions, selecting the appropriate medication, calculating the appropriate dosage, compounding, and providing instructions to the patient on how to take medications as prescribed by a licensed healthcare provider. The pharmacist or pharmacy technician is responsible for ensuring that the medication is accurately dispensed, and that the patient is informed about its use and potential side effects.
base it on ph pharmacy law

In the Philippines, Pharmacy Law or Republic Act No.10918 defines dispensing as the "preparation, packaging, labeling, or compounding necessary for the delivery of a drug to a patient or the patient's caregiver in a suitable container duly labeled for subsequent administration to, or use by, a patient, or the administering or delivery of a drug to a patient pursuant to a lawful order of a practitioner or other qualified prescriber." It is the responsibility of licensed pharmacist or licensed pharmacy technician to ensure that drugs are properly dispensed in accordance with the law and ethical standards. The law also mandates that the labeling of the dispensed drug must contain the name of the patient, the name of the prescribing practitioner, the directions for use, the date of dispensing, and any other information required by law.

Which of the following is acceptable as defense?
A. None of the choices
B. For the person to not know nor had any reason 
to believe that the cigarette or any other tobacco 
product was for the consumption of the minor to
whom it was sold.
C. For the person selling or distributing that he/she 
did not know or was not aware of the real age of 
the teenager

The answer is B. For the person to not know nor had any reason to believe that the cigarette or any other tobacco product was for the consumption of the minor to whom it was sold. According to Republic Act No. 10643, also known as the "Graphic Health Warning Law," it is unlawful for any person to sell, distribute, or purchase tobacco products to or from minors. However, a person who can prove that they did not know nor had any reason to believe that the tobacco product was for the consumption of a minor may use this as a valid defense. Knowing or being aware of the real age of the teenager is not an acceptable defense.

Which of the following is acceptable as defense?
A. None of the choices
B. For the person to not know nor had any reason 
to believe that the cigarette or any other tobacco 
product was for the consumption of the minor to
whom it was sold.
C. For the person selling or distributing that he/she 
did not know or was not aware of the real age of 
the teenager

ph law

It is important to specify which law or regulation is being referred to in order to provide an accurate answer. Could you please provide more context or information about the law or regulation being referred to in this question?

According to Philippine tobacco law (Republic Act No. 9211 or the Tobacco Regulation Act of 2003), the acceptable defense for the sale or distribution of tobacco products to minors is for the person to not have known nor had any reason to believe that the cigarette or any other tobacco product was for the consumption of the minor to whom it was sold. This defense is provided in Section 29 of the law, which states that "no person shall sell, distribute, or purchase any tobacco product to or from a minor. However, a person who can prove that he has reasonable cause to believe that the purchaser is above 18 years of age shall not be liable." This means that a seller or distributor who did not know or did not have reason to believe that the buyer or recipient of the tobacco product is a minor would not be held liable for violating the law.

The sale or distribution of tobacco products is 
prohibited within how many meters from any point 
of the perimeter of a school, public playground or 
other facility frequented particularly by minors?
a. 100
b. 25
c. 10
d. 50
e. 75
f. 200

According to the Philippine Tobacco Regulation Act of 2003 (Republic Act No. 9211), the sale or distribution of tobacco products is prohibited within 100 meters from any point of the perimeter of a school, public playground, or other facility frequented particularly by minors. This is stipulated in Section 6(a) of the law, which states that "no person or entity shall sell, distribute, or purchase any tobacco product within one hundred (100) meters from any point of the perimeter of a school, public playground or other facility frequented particularly by minors."
 
Therefore, the correct option is (a) 100.

Which of the following is not included in the 
exemptions for rooming-in and breastfeeding?
a. Infants whose mothers are seriously ill
b. Infants who have been delivered outside health 
institutions
c. Infants whose mothers are taking medications 
contraindicated to breast-feeding
d. Infants whose mothers are violent psychotics
e. Infants whose conditions do not permit roomingin and breast-feeding as determined by the 
attending physician
f. Infants whose mothers have conditions that do 
not permit breast-feeding and rooming-in as 
determined by the attending physician
g. None of the choices
There are no options presented to choose from. Please provide the options for a correct answer.
